title: Greek Village Salad

ingredients.name: ripe firm tomatoes, cut into wedges
green capsicum (shimla mirch), cut into rings

subs.name: If olives are unavailable, use pickled capers or simply leave them out — the salad is still excellent without them
Fresh firm paneer crumbled over the top gives a similar saline richness; or skip it entirely for a vegan version with the same dressing
A mild, good-quality oil can substitute, though extra virgin olive oil is central to the flavour here — the quantity is small so it is worth using the real thing
Fresh lemon juice in equal quantity adds a cleaner citrus brightness; both are traditional in different parts of Greece

steps.text: Cut the tomatoes into large wedges and the cucumber into thick half-moon chunks — they should look substantial and rustic, not finely diced. Slice the green capsicum into thin rings and the red onion into very thin half-moons.
Arrange the tomato, cucumber, capsicum, and red onion in a wide serving bowl or platter. Scatter the olives over the vegetables.

Place the block of feta on top of the salad — do not crumble it into the bowl. This is the traditional presentation and it looks far more impressive at the table. If using paneer, cut into a thick slab instead.

Drizzle the olive oil generously over everything, sprinkle the dried oregano on top (especially over the feta), and season with salt. Add a splash of red wine vinegar if you like more acidity. Gently toss the salad at the table just before eating so the oil coats everything.
For a proper Greek spread, warm pita bread or roti in a dry skillet over medium heat for 1 minute per side until warm and lightly charred in spots, then serve alongside the salad for scooping.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.

substitution_notes: cheese → Fresh firm paneer crumbled over the top gives a similar saline richness; or skip it entirely for a vegan version with the same dressing; olives → If olives are unavailable, use pickled capers or simply leave them out — the salad is still excellent without them; vinegar → Fresh lemon juice in equal quantity adds a cleaner citrus brightness; both are traditional in different parts of Greece; olive-oil → A mild, good-quality oil can substitute, though extra virgin olive oil is central to the flavour here — the quantity is small so it is worth using the real thing
indian_kitchen_adaptation: All vegetables here are completely standard in Indian markets year-round. Use shimla mirch for capsicum, any firm garden tomato for the tomatoes, and regular cucumbers. Feta is available at larger supermarkets and specialty cheese shops in metro cities; crumbled fresh paneer is a workable substitute with a similar mild, milky flavour. Good olive oil makes the biggest difference in this recipe since it is also the dressing — any decent brand from a supermarket will do. Serve with warm roti or tandoori naan instead of pita.
